hypothesis: If a Transformer-based encoder–decoder trained with patch-based spectrum embeddings and a joint reconstruction loss across multiple concentration ratios can predict the pure-component identities and their spectra from infrared spectra of mixtures (binary and ternary), then it will achieve high Top-1/Top-5 accuracy and high Tanimoto similarity compared to ground-truth components, because the reconstruction objective aligns encoder representations with the pure-component spectral space and multitask exposure generalizes across mixture ratios.
